name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 21.07.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 21.07.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As on 18 July, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 708.31 lakh ha (including 55.16 lakh ha (105% of normal area) in Sugarcane) as compared to 680.38 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 64.6 percent of the normal area has been sown  under  the  Kharif  season  crops.  The  status  of  sowing  under  Rice,  Pulses  and  Coarse Cereals is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As on 18 July, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 708.31 lakh ha (including 55.16 lakh ha (105% of normal area) in Sugarcane) as compared to 680.38 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 64.6 percent of the normal area has been sown  under  the  Kharif  season  crops.  The  status  of  sowing  under  Rice,  Pulses  and  Coarse Cereals is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal over East and North East India, Chhattisgarh, East Madhya  Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, Karnataka, Madhya Maharashtra, parts of Gujarat and Coastal Andhra Pradesh.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal over East and North East India, Chhattisgarh, East Madhya  Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, Karnataka, Madhya Maharashtra, parts of Gujarat and Coastal Andhra Pradesh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From June 01 -July  20,  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:  (-)22%; (ii) Northwest India: 30%; (iii) Central India: 24%; and South Peninsula:(-) 6%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From June 01 -July  20,  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:  (-)22%; (ii) Northwest India: 30%; (iii) Central India: 24%; and South Peninsula:(-) 6%.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A fresh low pressure area is likely to form over North Bay of Bengal around 24 th  July, 2025. Heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  over  Sub-Himalayan  West  Bengal  and  Sikkim  and northeastern  states  during  20 th   -26 th   July;  Himachal  Pradesh  and  Uttarakhand  20 th   -24 th July and increase in rainfall over coastal Odisha from 23 rd  July.
texts.text: A fresh low pressure area is likely to form over North Bay of Bengal around 24 th  July, 2025. Heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  over  Sub-Himalayan  West  Bengal  and  Sikkim  and northeastern  states  during  20 th   -26 th   July;  Himachal  Pradesh  and  Uttarakhand  20 th   -24 th July and increase in rainfall over coastal Odisha from 23 rd  July.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Heavy to very heavy rainfall likely to continue over Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu during next 6-7 days with isolated extremely heavy falls over Kerala and Coastal Karnataka on 19 th  & 20 th  July.
texts.text: Heavy to very heavy rainfall likely to continue over Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu during next 6-7 days with isolated extremely heavy falls over Kerala and Coastal Karnataka on 19 th  & 20 th  July.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Near normal to above normal rainfall activity is likely over east, and northwest India and Southern Peninsula and normal to below over the remaining parts of the country.
texts.text: Near normal to above normal rainfall activity is likely over east, and northwest India and Southern Peninsula and normal to below over the remaining parts of the country.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Inputs Situation
texts.text: Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. Water Reservoir Status :  As on 17 th  July 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 104.36 Billion Cubic Meter, which is 57% of total live storage capacity (182.46 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 191 percent of last year's storage and 170 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status :  As on 17 th  July 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 104.36 Billion Cubic Meter, which is 57% of total live storage capacity (182.46 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 191 percent of last year's storage and 170 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6. Pests & Diseases: Intensity of pests and diseases, at some fields, has been reported to be above  Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Maize,  Soyabean  and  Groundnut  (White  grub)  in Maharashtra. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The  overall  intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases: Intensity of pests and diseases, at some fields, has been reported to be above  Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Maize,  Soyabean  and  Groundnut  (White  grub)  in Maharashtra. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The  overall  intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During the week (10 July - 16 July2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 11%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 th June  to  16 th July  2025)  is  higher  than LPA by 9%.
texts.text: During the week (10 July - 16 July2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 11%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 th June  to  16 th July  2025)  is  higher  than LPA by 9%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall,  near  normal  to  above  normal  rainfall  activity  is  likely  over  central  &  south Peninsular  India;  near  normal  over  most  parts  of  east  &  northeast  India;  below  normal rainfall activity is likely over most parts of northwest India during the week.
texts.text: Overall,  near  normal  to  above  normal  rainfall  activity  is  likely  over  central  &  south Peninsular  India;  near  normal  over  most  parts  of  east  &  northeast  India;  below  normal rainfall activity is likely over most parts of northwest India during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The Monsoon trough is active with its western end runs to the south of its normal position and eastern end north of its normal position at mean sea level. It is likely to shift northward during next 2 days.
texts.text: The Monsoon trough is active with its western end runs to the south of its normal position and eastern end north of its normal position at mean sea level. It is likely to shift northward during next 2 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil moisture as on 18 July 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major  parts  of  Gujarat,  Rajasthan,  M.P,  Odisha,  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand,  Southern  West Bengal, Haryana and Western & Bundelkhand region of U.P.
texts.text: Soil moisture as on 18 July 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major  parts  of  Gujarat,  Rajasthan,  M.P,  Odisha,  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand,  Southern  West Bengal, Haryana and Western & Bundelkhand region of U.P.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It was lower than average of past 9 years in Northern and Central parts of Bihar, Eastern parts  of  U.P.,  Northern  parts  of  West  Bengal,  Telangana,  Assam  and  in  parts  of  A.P., Karnataka and Maharashtra.

texts.text: Rice : About 176.68 lakh ha area coverage under Kharif Rice as compared to 157.21 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
